name: Dalt's Baked Potato Soup
recipeIngredient: 3 medium White or red waxy potatoes, Peeled and quartered
4 cups Chicken broth or 2 (14.5 Ounce) cans reduced-salt Chicken broth and 6 tablespoons water
1 cup Dried potato flakes (I used Martha White "Spud Flakes")
recipeInstructions:
1. Place potatoes in a large pot of cold water and bring to a boil. Boil gently about 20 minutes or until potatoes are tender. Drain, cook and cut into 1/2-inch dice (about 3 cups), set aside.
2. In a large, heavy saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the onions and turn the heat to low. Cook the onions, stirring occasionally, for 15 minutes without browning them.
3. Stir in flour and let bubble for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally. Raise the heat to medium and whisk in the chicken broth. When the broth is hot, whisk in the potato flakes and continue whisking until the mixture is smooth. Let simmer on low heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Whisk in the half-and-half and simmer an additional 10 minutes; do not broil. Stir in the seasonings and the potato cubes.
5. To serve, divide among 6 large soup bowls. Sprinkle the tops with the bacon, cheese, and chives.
